title: cistus × cheiranthoides var. pauanus
intro: Cistus × cheiranthoides var. pauanus, also known as Rock Rose, is a hybrid plant species within the rockrose family, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and woody stems. Traditionally, Rock Rose has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rock Rose include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce anxiety and stress, and support respiratory health, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, coughs, and bronchitis. Rock Rose is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by drying the flowers and leaves of the plant or infusing them in hot water.
